read-history-with-log-and-show.label: Read the History You Built
read-history-with-log-and-show.summary: Inspect the community-site history and committed file content without changing state.

sketch0.title: History Is a Sequence of Snapshots

try_read_history_with_log_and_show_sketc.title: Read the Three-Commit Timeline
try_read_history_with_log_and_show_sketc.prompt: Type `git log --oneline` and press Enter in `community-site/`. Read the newest commit first; do not change repository state.

help.concept: git log --oneline summarizes each recorded snapshot with its subject.
help.hint_1: The newest subject should be Space community footer.
help.hint_2: The oldest subject should be Start community site.

try_read_history_with_log_and_show_sketc.starterCode:
# Community Site

Local events and resources.
try_read_history_with_log_and_show_sketc.instructions: Type `git log --oneline` and press Enter in `community-site/`. Read the newest commit first; do not change repository state.

zoeskoul_setup_sh.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash set -eu rm -rf -- community-site mkdir -p -- community-site git -C community-site init -q -b main git -C community-site config --local user.name 'ZoeSkoul Learner' git -C community-site config --local user.email learner@zoeskoul.local mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s '# Community Site Local events and resources. ' > community-site/README.md mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s ' Community Site

Community Events

' > community-site/index.html mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s 'body { font-family: sans-serif; } footer { margin-top: 12px; } ' > community-site/styles.css mkdir -p -- community-site/notes printf %s 'Archive the spring event list. ' > community-site/notes/old-events.txt mkdir -p -- community-site/dist printf %s 'console.log('"'"'legacy preview'"'"'); ' > community-site/dist/legacy-preview.js git -C community-site add -A GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-01T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-01T12:00:00Z git -C community-site commit -q -m 'Start community site' mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s ' Community Site

Community Events and Resources

' > community-site/index.html git -C community-site add -A GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-02T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-02T12:00:00Z git -C community-site commit -q -m 'Update community heading' mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s 'body { font-family: sans-serif; } footer { margin-top: 24px; } ' > community-site/styles.css git -C community-site add -A GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-03T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-03T12:00:00Z git -C community-site commit -q -m 'Space community footer'

' > community-site/README.md mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s ' Community Site

' > community-site/index.html mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s 'body { font-family: sans-serif; } footer { margin-top: 12px; } ' > community-site/styles.css mkdir -p -- community-site/notes printf %s 'Archive the spring event list. ' > community-site/notes/old-events.txt mkdir -p -- community-site/dist printf %s 'console.log('"'"'legacy preview'"'"'); ' > community-site/dist/legacy-preview.js git -C community-site add -A GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-01T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-01T12:00:00Z git -C community-site commit -q -m 'Start community site' mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s ' Community Site

Community Events and Resources

' > community-site/index.html git -C community-site add -A GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-02T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-02T12:00:00Z git -C community-site commit -q -m 'Update community heading' mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s 'body { font-family: sans-serif; } footer { margin-top: 24px; } ' > community-site/styles.css git -C community-site add -A GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-03T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-03T12:00:00Z git -C community-site commit -q -m 'Space community footer'

0.message: That action can discard or rewrite history. Use the safe, layer-specific workflow taught in this course.

try_read_history_with_log_and_show_sketc.title: Inspect the Latest Snapshot Summary
try_read_history_with_log_and_show_sketc.prompt: Type `git show --stat --oneline HEAD` and press Enter. Confirm that the latest snapshot changed styles.css.
try_read_history_with_log_and_show_sketc.hint: Ask Git to show the current HEAD commit with a file summary.

help.concept: HEAD identifies the currently checked-out commit.
help.hint_1: The subject should be Space community footer.
help.hint_2: The stat summary should name styles.css.

try_read_history_with_log_and_show_sketc.instructions: Type `git show --stat --oneline HEAD` and press Enter. Confirm that the latest snapshot changed styles.css.

0.message: That action can discard or rewrite history. Use the safe, layer-specific workflow taught in this course.

try_read_history_with_log_and_show_sketc.title: Read a File from the Latest Snapshot
try_read_history_with_log_and_show_sketc.prompt: Type `git show HEAD:styles.css` and press Enter. Confirm the committed file contains `footer { margin-top: 24px; }`.
try_read_history_with_log_and_show_sketc.hint: Use the commit-and-path form of git show.

help.concept: git show can read a file exactly as it exists in a committed snapshot.
help.hint_1: HEAD is the latest community-site commit.
help.hint_2: The path is styles.css relative to the repository root.

try_read_history_with_log_and_show_sketc.instructions: Type `git show HEAD:styles.css` and press Enter. Confirm the committed file contains `footer { margin-top: 24px; }`.

single-choice-1.title: HEAD in Git
single-choice-1.prompt: What does `HEAD` identify in this course?
single-choice-1.hint: Focus on what the command or concept observes or changes at this point in the workflow.

help.concept: Scan the repository timeline, open one checkpoint, understand `HEAD`, and move between overview and detail.
help.hint_1: Remove options that act on a different repository layer than the one named in the question.
help.hint_2: Choose the answer that remains accurate even when other unrelated files exist.

multi-choice-1.title: Understanding git log --oneline
multi-choice-1.hint: Choose the one answer that describes this command or concept precisely.

help.concept: Scan the repository timeline, open one checkpoint, understand `HEAD`, and move between overview and detail.
help.hint_1: Remove choices that act on a different repository layer or promise behavior Git does not provide.
help.hint_2: Prefer the exact definition used in this chapter over a nearby Git idea.

options.a: A full line-by-line diff for every commit
options.b: A compact one-line summary of each commit
options.c: Only the files currently in the staging area
options.d: Only the untracked files in the working tree

multi-choice-2.title: Purpose of git show
multi-choice-2.prompt: What is the primary purpose of the `git show` command?
multi-choice-2.hint: Choose the one answer that describes this command or concept precisely.

help.concept: Scan the repository timeline, open one checkpoint, understand `HEAD`, and move between overview and detail.
help.hint_1: Remove choices that act on a different repository layer or promise behavior Git does not provide.
help.hint_2: Prefer the exact definition used in this chapter over a nearby Git idea.

options.a: To list every commit as one short line
options.b: To display the details of a selected commit
options.c: To initialize an ordinary project folder

drag-reorder-1.title: Order of Git Commands
drag-reorder-1.prompt: Arrange the following Git commands in the order you would use them to inspect a commit's history and details.
drag-reorder-1.hint: Start with the state that must exist before the next action can make sense.

help.concept: Scan the repository timeline, open one checkpoint, understand `HEAD`, and move between overview and detail.
help.hint_1: Track how the project moves from working files to a proposed snapshot and then to history.
help.hint_2: Read the ordered steps as one continuous repository story.

sketch0.title: History Is a Sequence of Snapshots
sketch0.bodyMarkdown: `git log --oneline` lists the newest commit first. The short identifier is useful for reference, but the course never grades exact hashes because they can differ across valid repositories.

sketch1.title: HEAD Names the Current Snapshot
sketch1.bodyMarkdown: `HEAD` points to the commit currently checked out. `git show --stat --oneline HEAD` combines its subject with a compact file summary.

sketch2.title: Show Can Read Committed Content
sketch2.bodyMarkdown: `git show HEAD:path` reads a file from the committed snapshot without changing the working tree. History inspection should leave status unchanged.
